title: cirsium eriophorum subsp. spathulatum
intro: Cirsium eriophorum subsp. spathulatum, also known as the Siskiyou mariposa tulip, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. This plant is a perennial herb with bright yellow or orange flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Siskiyou mariposa tulip has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Cirsium eriophorum subsp. spathulatum include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
